title: Bath: Bath Marina & Bristol Cycle Path

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Charles, Eve + Tonic left with our 3 bikes (Simon's + Eve's new collapsible bikes) for Bath; we want to go on the Bath/Bristol cycle path.

excursions.label: Cycle ride to Bitton railway station (Bath/Bristol cycle path)

stops.excerpt: We cycled to Bitton railway station, where they have steam trains. It was 11 miles around trip.

excursions.label: Simon & Charles cycle into Bath to scout bike parking

stops.excerpt: In the afternoon Simon + Charles + Tonic set off to Bath to see where to tie up bikes the next day.

excursions.label: Bus trip into Bath: museums, Abbey, Sally Lunn

stops.excerpt: We saw the William Herschel Museum in a small Georgian town house. He lived with his sister, Caroline, who was also an astronomer.

Then we saw the Micromuseum where there are tiny sculptures by Willard [illegible] Adams & Eve carved into a pencil point & the statue of Liberty in the eye of a needle, viewed through magnifying glasses.
We had a cup of tea & looked at Bath Abbey. Nice embroidered Plaques show links to West Indies. Pale stone.

stops.normalized_name: Sally Lunn's Historic Eating House, Bath, UK
stops.excerpt: trencher supper at Sally Lunn on a Sally Lunn bun. The basement shows floor levels from Roman times.

boundary_flags: mixed_end: page A_0292_L is shared with the next trip; the diary text ends mid-sentence ('Simon') and the remaining content belongs to the following trip
summary: A May bank holiday weekend trip for Simon, Eve, Charles and dog Tonic to Bath Marina campsite, using the Bath/Bristol cycle path. They cycled 11 miles to Bitton railway station on Saturday, and on Sunday took the bus into Bath to visit the William Herschel Museum, a Micromuseum, Bath Abbey, and Sally Lunn's historic eating house.
extraction_notes: Only three family members attended (Simon, Eve, Charles); no ferry or border crossings — entirely UK. The end boundary is mixed (A_0292_L), with the text cutting off mid-sentence; remainder of that page belongs to the next trip. The Bristol cycle path is the motivation/route cycled rather than a named stop. The 'Tonic' mentioned in the diary is the dog (also called 'Tinder' elsewhere in the family context; here named Tonic throughout). Date_end is inferred as 2001-05-06 from the 'May 6th' heading; return to Birmingham is not narrated but assumed per standard trip pattern.
